summary: Reporter expects SKImage.Encode to work with BMP, GIF, ICO, WBMP, PKM, KTX, ASTC, DNG, HEIF, and AVIF formats, but Skia only implements encoders for JPEG, PNG, and WebP — all other formats return null by design.

bugSignals.errorMessage: SKImage.Encode returns null for unsupported formats (BMP, GIF, ICO, WBMP, PKM, KTX, ASTC, DNG, HEIF, AVIF)

stepsToReproduce: Create an SKBitmap and draw on it
Result is null; calling SaveTo on null throws NullReferenceException

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The encoding limitation is unchanged in current versions — SKPixmap.Encode still only supports JPEG, PNG, and WebP.

analysis.summary: SKPixmap.Encode uses a switch expression that only handles JPEG, PNG, and WebP formats. All other SKEncodedImageFormat values hit the default case returning false, which surfaces as null from the public API. This is an upstream Skia limitation — Skia does not ship encoders for BMP, GIF, ICO, HEIF, AVIF, etc. The enum includes these values because Skia can decode them, not encode. Issue #320 reported the same for BMP in 2017 and maintainer @mattleibow confirmed Skia only compiles JPEG/PNG/WebP encoders.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug with low confidence because the behavior is by design (Skia limitation), but the SKEncodedImageFormat enum is misleading — it suggests encoding support for formats that only have decoders. The reporter's expectation is reasonable. Suggesting close-as-by-design since the fix would require Skia to add new encoders upstream, which is out of SkiaSharp's control.

keySignals.text: encountered an issue while converting to SKData for specific formats such as BMP, GIF, ICO, WBMP, PKM, KTX, ASTC, DNG, HEIF, and AVIF
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter lists exactly the formats without Skia encoders.

keySignals.text: This issue does not occur when using formats such as JPG, PNG, and WEBP
keySignals.interpretation: These are the three formats that DO have encoders — confirms the known limitation.

keySignals.text: This is not a bug. The encodings for those formats are not implemented by Skia.
keySignals.interpretation: Community member correctly identified the root cause.

codeInvestigation.finding: The switch expression in Encode(SKWStream, SKEncodedImageFormat, int) only handles Jpeg, Png, and Webp. All other enum values fall through to '_ => false', returning null to callers.

SKImage.Encode delegates to SKPixmap.Encode via PeekPixels(). When SKPixmap.Encode returns null, SKImage.Encode also returns null — the reporter then gets a NullReferenceException calling SaveTo on the null result.

SKEncodedImageFormat enum lists 13 formats (Bmp through Avif), but only 3 have encoder implementations. The enum mirrors Skia's SkEncodedImageFormat which is used for both decoding and encoding identification.

workarounds: Use SKEncodedImageFormat.Png, Jpeg, or Webp instead — these are the only formats with encoder support
For BMP output, use a third-party library like BmpSharp to convert from PNG/raw pixel data

resolution.hypothesis: Skia only includes encoders for JPEG, PNG, and WebP. The SKEncodedImageFormat enum contains values for formats that only have decoder support, making it misleading when used with Encode methods.

proposals.title: Use a supported encoding format
proposals.description: Switch to SKEncodedImageFormat.Png, Jpeg, or Webp. These are the only three formats Skia implements encoders for. PNG is lossless, WebP supports both lossy and lossless, and JPEG is lossy.

proposals.title: Add null check before SaveTo
proposals.description: Guard against null return from Encode to avoid NullReferenceException, and handle unsupported formats gracefully.

resolution.recommendedReason: Simplest fix — just change the format enum. PNG is a drop-in replacement for most use cases requiring lossless output.

actionability.reason: Skia only implements JPEG/PNG/WebP encoders. This is a well-documented upstream limitation confirmed by maintainer on #320. The SKEncodedImageFormat enum is shared between decoding and encoding, which is misleading but by design.

actions.description: Apply by-design and core area labels
Explain by-design behavior and provide workaround
